visa_types.processing_time:
Processing times in Dominican Republic
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within 15 working days from the date your application is submitted in the Visa Application Center.
When all documentation is complete and meets all the requirements your application  will be send to  the Embassy of Italy in Panama.
Important notes:
- Visa applications are assessed individually and  processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application  will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your  travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Spain until you have  received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Embassy of Italy in Panama, for 60 days for processing of visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview in the Visa Application Center, if the Embassy of Italy in Panama requires it
When to prepare your application
The Embassy of Italy in Panama advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months (90 days) in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service Centre by using the details available on the Contact Us page of this website.
Role of Visa Application Centre in processing
Please note Visa application Centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy of Italy in Panama.

## Overview

## Visa Fees
This section contains information about visa application fees and service charges, if you are applying for a visa at the visa service center to come to Italy from Dominican Republic.
Dependents are charged the same fee as the main applicant unless otherwise stated. All dependents that are travelling must pay the application fee and service charges whether or not they are included in the main applicant's passport with some exception. 
You can use the quick links listed here to take you the  information or you can scroll down the page. 
- Visa Fee
- Service Charges
- Optional services
- Courier Domesa Service Cost ( Additional Service )
- Visa Fees waived categories
- Refund process

## Visa Fees
The below rates apply at the visa service centres. They may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash only. Credit cards and other forms of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.

[ita > dom > en > Table:Business (Type C Only) > Visa Fees 1]
 The payment of the consular fee must be done in the application center only. Applicants must not make any payment in the bank acounts of the embassy of italy. Embassy will not make any refund of the consular fees paid erroneously in the embassy bank account 

## Service Charges

[ita > dom > en > Table:Business (Type C Only) > Visa Fees 2]
 Please Note: 
- Service charges are inclusive of VAT levied per application over and above the visa fees.

## Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added service. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website. 

## Courier  Service Cost ( Additional Service )
- In Santo Domingo:   $16.50
- Outside Santo Domingo $24

## Visa Fees waived categories
- Children under six years – Only Schengen Visa type (A or C)
- Study visa applicants
- Researchers traveling for the purpose of scientific research as defined in Recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and the Council of 28 September 2005 in order to facilitate the issue by Member States of uniform for short to researchers from third countries traveling within the Community for purposes of scientific research;
- Blood relatives of citizens of the European Union within the second degree of kinship. (Direct ancestors: parents, grandparents, great grandparents…; Descendants: children, grandchildren, great-grandchildren…, siblings and spouse). (To obtain this benefit, on the day of interview must demonstrate unequivocal way kinship, exhibiting civil status registers).
Note: Please note that you may be charged visa fee even though you meet the above information. This will be done only in consultation with the Consular Department of the Embassy of Italy in Dominican Republic

## Refund process
Visa application fee reflects the administrative costs of processing an application. Your visa application fee will not be refunded unless:
- You withdraw your application in writing before any processing takes place; or
- Embassy authorizes a refund
Please note that visa fee and service charges are not refunded if your application is refused by the Embassy of Italy, or if you withdraw you application while it is under processing at the Embassy of Italy in Dominican Republic.

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please
click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/dom/en/ita/travel-insurance)

## Overview
When applying for a visa,  you are required to bring 01 photograph as part of completing and submitting  your visa application.

## Photo Requirements

You need to submit 1 (One)  photograph. The photo must be pasted (not stapled) on the visa application  form. Your visa application photo must meet certain criteria regarding size and  content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa  application process. Your photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the  image below:

Head Size
Your head height, measured from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must be between 50% and 70% of the photo's total height. Your eye height, measured from the bottom of the photo to the level of your eyes, should be between 55% and 70% - or roughly 2/3 - of the photo's height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 3cm x  4cm and square-shaped, meaning the photo's height must be the same as its  width.

## 7 Steps to Successful Photo
Head Orientation
Head orientation is  important when taking your visa photo. Frame yourself within the photo so that  your full face shows. Face directly toward the camera and make sure your eyes  are open.
Fill the Frame
Your photo must show your  entire head, from the top of your hair to the bottom of your chin.

Stay Centered
Center your head within the  frame.
Show Your Eyes
Make sure your eyes are  open.
Background & Quality
The area behind you in the  photo should be uncluttered and either white. Sit in front of a plain white  background for best results. Photo quality must be of high resolution and must  be printed on normal photographic paper (photo printing)
Eliminate Shadows
Sit so that your face is  fully-lit and there are no distracting shadows across your face or in the  background
Relax and Look Natural
Be sure to have a natural  expression on your face when you take your photo, like those shown here:

## More Information
For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here

## Processing Time
Processing times in Dominican Republic
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within 15 working days from the date your application is submitted in the Visa Application Center.
When all documentation is complete and meets all the requirements your application  will be send to  the Embassy of Italy in Panama.
Important notes:
- Visa applications are assessed individually and  processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application  will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your  travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Spain until you have  received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Embassy of Italy in Panama, for 60 days for processing of visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview in the Visa Application Center, if the Embassy of Italy in Panama requires it
When to prepare your application
The Embassy of Italy in Panama advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months (90 days) in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service Centre by using the details available on the Contact Us page of this website.
Role of Visa Application Centre in processing
Please note Visa application Centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy of Italy in Panama.

The visa must be requested  personally in Dominican Republic, with documentation that must complete as  described below. 
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED FOR ANY  INDEPENDENT WORK INDICATED BELOW 
- VISA APPLICATION FORM FULLY COMPLETED. (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1LMJcZ4NIxyImxvoyhPClc/1682a74062137f11a999d7fca8305d67/national_d.pdf)

- STUDY TITLE COPY AUTHENTICATED BY THE MINISTRY  OF NATIONAL EDUCATION.
- Photocopy of the first page of the passport. -
(Sheet with photography and personal  data only)
- Receipt of payments of visa fee.
- Original and photocopy of
the citizenship ID
of  applicants who are at least 18 years of age.
- Passport
valid for at least 6  months from the date of travel.
Freelancer 
In addition to the  requirements of paragraph 1 to paragraph 9: 
- Certification of the entity granting  authorization to practice as a freelancer.
- Suitable accommodation provided by Italian law.
- Proof of income relative to the last year of  work for a minimum of Euro 8.400
INDEPENDENT WORK FOR A  COMPANY IN ITALY 
In addition to the  requirements of paragraph 1 to paragraph 9: 
- Employment contract affording workers  compensation above the minimum wage Under Italian law.
- Last stock of the Company or last tax return to  ensure the remuneration expressed in point 1.
- Certificate of registration with the Chamber of  Commerce to show that the Company is active for at least three years.
- Copy of the statement of responsibility sent to  the "Direzione Provinciale del Lavoro, Servizio Ispezione del Lavoro"  of the Italian city of residence of the employer stating that will not be  implemented to any subordinate work.
INDEPENDENT WORK AS  PARTNERS, DIRECTORS OF COMPANIES IN ITALY 
In addition to the  requirements of paragraph 1 to paragraph 9: 
- Certificate of registration with the Chamber of  Commerce to show that the Company is active for at least three years.
- Statement by the manager of the Italian company  to guarantee the worker a salary above the minimum wage Under Italian law.
- Last stock of the Company or last tax return to  ensure the payment of wages under Italian law
- Copy of the statement of responsibility sent to  the "Direzione Provinciale del Lavoro, Servizio Ispezione del Lavoro"  of the Italian city of residence of the employer stating that will not be  implemented to any subordinate work.
NOTE: Failure to submit one  of the documents listed is the rejection of the visa application. The Embassy  reserves the right to request additional documentation as per the case.

## Photo Specifications
- Overview
- Photo Requirements
- 7 Steps to Successful Photo
- Photograph facility at VAC
- More Information

## Overview
When applying for a visa,  you are required to bring 01 photograph as part of completing and submitting  your visa application.

## Photo Requirements

You need to submit 1 (One)  photograph. The photo must be pasted (not stapled) on the visa application  form. Your visa application photo must meet certain criteria regarding size and  content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa  application process. Your photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the  image below:

Head Size
Your head height, measured from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must be between 50% and 70% of the photo's total height. Your eye height, measured from the bottom of the photo to the level of your eyes, should be between 55% and 70% - or roughly 2/3 - of the photo's height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 3cm x  4cm and square-shaped, meaning the photo's height must be the same as its  width.

## 7 Steps to Successful Photo
Head Orientation
Head orientation is  important when taking your visa photo. Frame yourself within the photo so that  your full face shows. Face directly toward the camera and make sure your eyes  are open.
Fill the Frame
Your photo must show your  entire head, from the top of your hair to the bottom of your chin.

Stay Centered
Center your head within the  frame.
Show Your Eyes
Make sure your eyes are  open.
Background & Quality
The area behind you in the  photo should be uncluttered and either white. Sit in front of a plain white  background for best results. Photo quality must be of high resolution and must  be printed on normal photographic paper (photo printing)
Eliminate Shadows
Sit so that your face is  fully-lit and there are no distracting shadows across your face or in the  background
Relax and Look Natural
Be sure to have a natural  expression on your face when you take your photo, like those shown here:

## More Information
For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here

## Processing Time
Processing times in Dominican Republic
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within 15 working days from the date your application is submitted to the Visa Application Center.
When all documentation is complete and meets all the requirements your application will  be send to the Embassy of Italy in Panama.
Important notes:
- Visa applications are assessed individually and  processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application  will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your  travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Spain until you have  received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Embassy of  Italy in Dominican Republic, Dominican Republic for 60 days for processing of  visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview in the Visa Application Center, if the Embassy of Italy in requires it
When to prepare your application
The Embassy of Italy in advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months (90 days) in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service Centre by using the details available on the Contact Us page of this website.
Role of Visa Application Centre in processing
Please note Visa application Centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy of Italy in Panama.

## Overview
When applying for a visa,  you are required to bring 01 photograph as part of completing and submitting  your visa application.

## Photo Requirements

You need to submit 1 (One)  photograph. The photo must be pasted (not stapled) on the visa application  form. Your visa application photo must meet certain criteria regarding size and  content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa  application process. Your photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the  image below:

Head Size
Your head height, measured from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must be between 50% and 70% of the photo's total height. Your eye height, measured from the bottom of the photo to the level of your eyes, should be between 55% and 70% - or roughly 2/3 - of the photo's height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 3cm x  4cm and square-shaped, meaning the photo's height must be the same as its  width.

## 7 Steps to Successful Photo
Head Orientation
Head orientation is  important when taking your visa photo. Frame yourself within the photo so that  your full face shows. Face directly toward the camera and make sure your eyes  are open.
Fill the Frame
Your photo must show your  entire head, from the top of your hair to the bottom of your chin.

Stay Centered
Center your head within the  frame.
Show Your Eyes
Make sure your eyes are  open.
Background & Quality
The area behind you in the  photo should be uncluttered and either white. Sit in front of a plain white  background for best results. Photo quality must be of high resolution and must  be printed on normal photographic paper (photo printing)
Eliminate Shadows
Sit so that your face is  fully-lit and there are no distracting shadows across your face or in the  background
Relax and Look Natural
Be sure to have a natural  expression on your face when you take your photo, like those shown here:

## More Information
For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here

## Processing Time
Processing times in Dominican Republic
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within 15 working days from the date your application is submitted to the Embassy of Italy in Panama.
When all documentation is complete and meets all the requirements  your application will be send to the Embassy of Italy in Panama
Important notes:
- Visa applications are assessed individually and  processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application  will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your  travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Spain until you have  received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Embassy of Italy in Panama for 60 days for processing of visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview in the Visa Application Center , if the Embassy of Italy in Panama requires it
When to prepare your application
The Embassy of Italy in Panama advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months (90 days) in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service Centre by using the details available on the Contact Us page of this website.
Role of Visa Application Centre in processing
Please note Visa application Centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy of Italy in Panama.

ATENTION
With the intention that you project your travel, in case of get your study visa the embasy only give 8 days before at start program.            
          	
Based on the new rules of the Italian Ministry of Education (MIUR), study visa for single inmatriculación undergraduate courses Universities and Italian AFAM institutions, can be given to children who have reached 17 years of age. This rule does not apply to the years following the inmatriculación  The study visa may also be issued to minors who have reached 14 years of age participating in exchange programs and cultural initiatives promoted by specialized agencies (eg. AFS Intercultural Programs) have been explicitly authorized by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs,
            
Otherwise the student visa can not be issued to minors
            
WARNING: students interested in university study in Italian universities the MIUR (Ministry of University Italy) required at time of pre-registration for the first year a good knowledge of the Italian language, equivalent to level B2 the Common European Framework.            
            
It further specifies that for any pre-registration for the first and subsequent years for postgraduate courses (Masters, PhD Specializations and Research) is generally required by the universities themselves an adequate knowledge of the language equivalent to the level above.            
            
Without this knowledge, you cannot make these courses in Italy. Therefore it is suggested that interested students to advance studies of the Italian language with a minimum of six (6) months in advance of the dates of pre-college enrollment.            
            
For further information on this warning can contact the Istituto Italiano di Cultura, Cultural Office of the Italian Embassy to 2453824-2325195-2321954 numbers and request a meeting with advisers from Italy.
          
If your study involves agreements between universities, exchange or double degree certification please present the corresponding certification of you school in Colombia in which it specifies the type of agreement, duration and other details that support the agreement.

- You withdraw your application in writing before any processing takes place; or
- Embassy authorizes a refund
Please note that visa fee and service charges are not refunded if your application is refused by the Embassy of Italy, or if you withdraw you application while it is under processing at the Embassy of Italy in Dominican Republic.

## Overview
When applying for a visa,  you are required to bring 01 photograph as part of completing and submitting  your visa application.

## Photo Requirements

You need to submit 1 (One)  photograph. The photo must be pasted (not stapled) on the visa application  form. Your visa application photo must meet certain criteria regarding size and  content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa  application process. Your photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the  image below:

Head Size
Your head height, measured from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must be between 50% and 70% of the photo's total height. Your eye height, measured from the bottom of the photo to the level of your eyes, should be between 55% and 70% - or roughly 2/3 - of the photo's height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 3cm x  4cm and square-shaped, meaning the photo's height must be the same as its  width.

## 7 Steps to Successful Photo
Head Orientation
Head orientation is  important when taking your visa photo. Frame yourself within the photo so that  your full face shows. Face directly toward the camera and make sure your eyes  are open.
Fill the Frame
Your photo must show your  entire head, from the top of your hair to the bottom of your chin.

Stay Centered
Center your head within the  frame.
Show Your Eyes
Make sure your eyes are  open.
Background & Quality
The area behind you in the  photo should be uncluttered and either white. Sit in front of a plain white  background for best results. Photo quality must be of high resolution and must  be printed on normal photographic paper (photo printing)
Eliminate Shadows
Sit so that your face is  fully-lit and there are no distracting shadows across your face or in the  background
Relax and Look Natural
Be sure to have a natural  expression on your face when you take your photo, like those shown here:

## More Information
For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here

## Processing Time
Processing times in Dominican Republic
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within15working days from the date your application is submitted to the Embassy of Italy in Panama
When all documentation is complete and meets all the requirements your application will be send to  the Embassy of Italy in Panama
Important notes:
- Visa applications are assessed individually and  processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application  will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your  travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Spain until you have  received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Embassy of Italy in Panama for 60 days for processing of visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview in the Visa application Center, if the Embassy of Italy in Panama requires it
When to prepare your application
The Embassy of Italy in Panama advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months (90 days) in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service Centre by using the details available on the Contact Us page of this website.
Role of Visa Application Centre in processing
Please note Visa application Centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy of Italy in Panama.

## Overview

## Visa Fees
This section contains information about visa application fees and service charges, if you are applying for a visa at the visa service center to come to Italy from Dominican Republic.
Dependents are charged the same fee as the main applicant unless otherwise stated. All dependents that are travelling must pay the application fee and service charges whether or not they are included in the main applicant's passport with some exception. 
You can use the quick links listed here to take you the  information or you can scroll down the page.

## Visa Fees
The below rates apply at the visa service centres. They may be varied at any time. Charges are non-refundable and may only be paid in cash only. Credit cards and other forms of payment are not accepted. Whenever possible the cashier should be presented with the exact money.

## Optional value added services
To add value to our customer, we offer some value added service. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee when you attend the visa service centre only. To know about these services please refer the additional service page of this website. 

## Courier  Service Cost ( Additional Service )

- Outside Santo Domingo $24

## Visa Fees waived categories
- Children under six years – Only Schengen Visa type (A or C)
- Study visa applicants
- Researchers traveling for the purpose of scientific research as defined in Recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and the Council of 28 September 2005 in order to facilitate the issue by Member States of uniform for short to researchers from third countries traveling within the Community for purposes of scientific research;

(Direct ancestors: parents, grandparents, great grandparents…; Descendants: children, grandchildren, great-grandchildren…, siblings and spouse). ## Overview
When applying for a visa,  you are required to bring 01 photograph as part of completing and submitting  your visa application.

## Photo Requirements

You need to submit 1 (One)  photograph. The photo must be pasted (not stapled) on the visa application  form. Your visa application photo must meet certain criteria regarding size and  content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa  application process. Your photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the  image below:

Head Size
Your head height, measured from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must be between 50% and 70% of the photo's total height. Your eye height, measured from the bottom of the photo to the level of your eyes, should be between 55% and 70% - or roughly 2/3 - of the photo's height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 3cm x  4cm and square-shaped, meaning the photo's height must be the same as its  width.

## 7 Steps to Successful Photo
Head Orientation
Head orientation is  important when taking your visa photo. Frame yourself within the photo so that  your full face shows. Face directly toward the camera and make sure your eyes  are open.
Fill the Frame
Your photo must show your  entire head, from the top of your hair to the bottom of your chin.

Stay Centered
Center your head within the  frame.
Show Your Eyes
Make sure your eyes are  open.
Background & Quality
The area behind you in the  photo should be uncluttered and either white. Sit in front of a plain white  background for best results. Photo quality must be of high resolution and must  be printed on normal photographic paper (photo printing)
Eliminate Shadows
Sit so that your face is  fully-lit and there are no distracting shadows across your face or in the  background
Relax and Look Natural
Be sure to have a natural  expression on your face when you take your photo, like those shown here:

## Processing Time
Processing times in Dominican Republic
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within 15 working days from the date your application is submitted to the Embassy of Italy in Panama.
When all documentation is complete and meets all the requirements your application will be send to the Embassy in Panama
Important notes:
- Visa applications are assessed individually and  processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application  will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your  travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Spain until you have  received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Embassy of Italy in Panama for 60 days for processing of visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview in the Visa Application Center , if the Embassy of Italy in Panama requires it
When to prepare your application
The Embassy of Italy in Panama advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months (90 days) in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service Centre by using the details available on the Contact Us page of this website.
Role of Visa Application Centre in processing
Please note Visa application Centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy of Italy in Panama

## Overview
When applying for a visa,  you are required to bring 01 photograph as part of completing and submitting  your visa application.

## Photo Requirements

You need to submit 1 (One)  photograph. The photo must be pasted (not stapled) on the visa application  form. Your visa application photo must meet certain criteria regarding size and  content. Submitting photos that do not meet these criteria can delay the visa  application process. Your photographs must meet the guidelines shown in the  image below:

Head Size
Your head height, measured from the top of your head, including the hair, to the bottom of your chin, must be between 50% and 70% of the photo's total height. Your eye height, measured from the bottom of the photo to the level of your eyes, should be between 55% and 70% - or roughly 2/3 - of the photo's height.
Photo Dimensions
Your photo must be 3cm x  4cm and square-shaped, meaning the photo's height must be the same as its  width.

## 7 Steps to Successful Photo
Head Orientation
Head orientation is  important when taking your visa photo. Frame yourself within the photo so that  your full face shows. Face directly toward the camera and make sure your eyes  are open.
Fill the Frame
Your photo must show your  entire head, from the top of your hair to the bottom of your chin.

Stay Centered
Center your head within the  frame.
Show Your Eyes
Make sure your eyes are  open.
Background & Quality
The area behind you in the  photo should be uncluttered and either white. Sit in front of a plain white  background for best results. Photo quality must be of high resolution and must  be printed on normal photographic paper (photo printing)
Eliminate Shadows
Sit so that your face is  fully-lit and there are no distracting shadows across your face or in the  background
Relax and Look Natural
Be sure to have a natural  expression on your face when you take your photo, like those shown here:

## More Information
For more information about visa application photo specifications, visit click here

## Processing Time
Processing times in Dominican Republic
The Majority of standard visa applications are processed within 15 working days from the date your application is submitted to the Embassy of Italy in Panama
When all documentation is complete and meets all the requirements your application will be send to  the Embassy in Panama
Important notes:
- Visa applications are assessed individually and  processing times may vary. The actual processing time of your visa application  will depend on several factors and because of this you should not to make your  travel arrangements to enter or transit through the Spain until you have  received your Schengen visa.
- Your passport can be retained by the Embassy of Italy in Panama for 60 days for processing of visa application
- You may require to appear for personal interview in the Visa Application Center , if the Embassy of Italy in Panama requires it
When to prepare your application
The Embassy of Italy in Panama advises you to allow enough time to prepare your visa application. Some applicants can take 1 to 2 weeks to prepare their visa application and you should allow enough time to read the guidance on the visa category you are applying for, complete your visa application form, prepare your supporting documents and arrange for translations (if necessary). You can submit your visa application up to 3 months (90 days) in advance of your planned travel date.
Queries about processing times
You can track the status of your application by  using the Track your Application facility on this website and by using the SMS  text alert facility (fee payable).you can contact visa service Centre by using  the details available on the Contact Us page of this website.
Role of Visa Application Centre in processing
Please note Visa application Centre and none of its staff has any role in the decision making process. Processing of visa application is completed by the staffs at the Embassy of Italy in Panama.
